div.head: The Ambassador in Germany (Dodd) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
80. The Foreign Office has just informed us of the receipt of a communication from the British Government Documents on International Affairs, 1396, p. 90. assuring it of the British Government’s interest in a peaceful solution of the present situation and stating that any proposals made in London either by
the German Government or others should be discussed in due course. The German Government considers this the best that can be expected under the circumstances and has telegraphed Avenol that the German delegation will be in London Thursday morning, Ribbentrop to be the representative. He will be accompanied by Dieckhoff and Woermann of the Foreign Office, the latter being of the Legal Section.
These men represent different elements here and Dieckhoff is very able, liberal and well informed. This eases tension here a good deal.

The German Government realizes that the first days will not be pleasant and seems prepared to face this with the idea that the German Government will thereafter have the opportunity to present its proposals.
Will cable further important news when we are able to get any.
